title: Ear-EEG Sleep Monitoring 2023 (EESM23)
description: The Ear-EEG Sleep Monitoring 2023 (EESM23) dataset comprises nightly EEG recordings from 10 healthy participants collected during 2020-2022. The dataset includes polysomnography and ear-EEG measurements from the first two subjects, with subsequent recordings consisting of ear-EEG data alone. Each recording contains both a video task sequence performed prior to sleep and a sleep sequence recorded overnight in the subject's home environment, providing a resource for investigating ear-based EEG monitoring during sleep.
methods_description: EEG data were collected using ear-EEG equipment, with the first two recordings including concurrent polysomnography (PSG) measurements. For the initial two subjects, recording equipment was mounted in the afternoon by researchers; for remaining subjects, equipment was self-mounted immediately before sleep. All recordings took place in subjects' home environments. Subjects marked key timepoints (recording start, electrode test start, bedtime, lights-out, recording end) using trigger buttons on the equipment.
